--- IMAGES ---

IMAGE 1:
URL: https://3.bp.blogspot.com/-79RiItOAeHg/UIYrzGaA3mI/AAAAAAAAAQ4/u2J_lHJwXDU/s1600/FinalProjectMap.jpg
DESCRIPTION: The map displays the distribution and frequency of natural disasters in the US, including earthquakes, volcanic eruptions, hurricanes, and tornado tracks, with a focus on historical GIS studies and their impact.

IMAGE 2:
URL: https://www.igismap.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/03/Disaster-manage.jpg
DESCRIPTION: The graphic illustrates the concept of GIS in disaster management, featuring a stylized urban landscape alongside detailed maps showing the impact of the 2006 floods in the Horn of Africa, including travel times to key cities and the extent of flooding.

IMAGE 3:
URL: https://techgeo.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/GIS-natural-disasters.jpg
DESCRIPTION: A digital map overlay illustrating historical natural disaster impacts on a coastal urban area, with color-coded zones indicating different risk levels or affected regions, complemented by labels such as "Elsoen Merflor," "Sourry Foute," "Aster Nstry," and "Muta Loney George."

IMAGE 4:
URL: https://blog.richmond.edu/geog250/files/2017/12/Screen-Shot-2017-12-08-at-10.40.56-PM.png
DESCRIPTION: A world map depicting various seismic and volcanic activity locations, indicated by colored dots, highlighting natural disaster risks across several continents, including areas near Barcelona Province in Spain.

IMAGE 5:
URL: https://i.pinimg.com/originals/f7/44/9a/f7449af3559b1e633e6f6b284c0ed657.jpg
DESCRIPTION: A topographic map of Japan highlights earthquake aftershock magnitudes with purple circles and shows horizontal land shifts in red, orange, and yellow, indicating areas affected by a natural disaster as part of a historical GIS natural disaster mapping study.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

enriched_points.expanded_content: GIS tools enable comprehensive mapping of historical natural disasters, revealing patterns of frequency and impact across regions, aiding in future disaster preparedness.
Historical GIS allows researchers to analyze the spatial relationships of past settlements, illustrating how geography influenced habitation, resource access, and community development.
GIS applications help identify historical community responses to disasters, informing current resilience strategies and adaptation practices to minimize future risks and enhance recovery.
